22 April 2026 Military News

News Reports

Current Operations

    Other Operations

  • US Turns Off Dollar Tap to Squeeze Iraq Over Iran-Backed Militias Sputnik 22 Apr 2026 -- The United States has suspended shipments of US dollars to Iraq and frozen select security cooperation programs, stepping up pressure on Baghdad to clamp down on Iran-backed militias, The Wall Street Journal reported, citing Iraqi and American officials.

  • Statement From Chairman of the Afghanistan Withdrawal Special Review Panel Sean Parnell DoD 22 Apr 2026 -- Following the Afghanistan Withdrawal Special Review Panel's recommendation and at the direction of Secretary of War Pete Hegseth, the United States Marine Corps has upgraded the valor awards for the Marines of Company G, 2d Battalion, 1st Marines, who stood at Abbey Gate on 26 August 2021. After reviewing the original awards and determining that several had been inappropriately downgraded, these awards have now been upgraded to levels that more accurately reflect the extreme risk these Marines knowingly accepted and the lives they saved under direct enemy fire.

    • Operation Epic Fury / Operation Roaring Lion | Operation True Promise 4

  • Iran's Revolutionary Guards Say They Seized 2 Ships In Hormuz Strait RFE/RL 22 Apr 2026 -- Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps (IRGC) says it has seized "two offending vessels" during an alleged "secret attempt to leave" the Strait of Hormuz.
  • April 22: Iran tightens Strait of Hormuz control, intercepts hostile vessels, reinforces deterrence Press TV 22 Apr 2026 -- Iran consolidated its control over the Strait of Hormuz after the Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) Navy intercepted two hostile vessels linked to the Israeli regime and transferred them into Iranian territorial waters.
  • Data shows Iran bypassing US blockade despite Trump's claims of its success Press TV 22 Apr 2026 -- Data from a major ship tracking company shows that dozens of oil tankers have managed to bypass the so-called naval blockade of Iran by the United States since it began earlier this month.
  • IRGC seizes Israeli ship, second vessel for violations in Strait of Hormuz Press TV 22 Apr 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) Navy says it has intercepted and transferred to Iranian territorial waters two vessels for having committed violations in the Strait of Hormuz.

  • US Wants 'Unified' Iranian Proposal For Ending War, No Firm Deadline For Now RFE/RL 22 Apr 2026 -- US President Donald Trump wants to see a "unified" response from Tehran to US proposals for ending the US-Israeli war with Iran and has not set a "firm deadline" at this point, the White House said a day after he unilaterally extended a cease-fire that had been due to expire on April 22.
  • US Lawmakers, Experts Sound Alarm On Deadly Iran-Russia Axis RFE/RL 22 Apr 2026 -- In a political climate where bipartisan unity is increasingly rare, a consensus is forming among US lawmakers and foreign policy heavyweights: The relationship between Moscow and Tehran has moved far beyond diplomatic convenience.
  • Trump says US-Iran talks 'possible' within 36-72 hours Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 22 Apr 2026 -- President Donald Trump indicated Wednesday that a second round of US-Iran negotiations could take place within the next "36 to 72 hours," as Pakistan continues mediating between the two sides, according to Anadolu Agency.
  • U.S. announces to extend ceasefire, but peace talks stall as Iran reportedly decides to sit out People's Daily 22 Apr 2026 -- The process of the expected second round of peace talks between the United States and Iran seems stalled as Iran reportedly decided not to take part, accusing the United States of violating the ceasefire. Meanwhile, U.S. President Donald Trump announced to extend the ceasefire on some conditions.
  • US extends Iran ceasefire before truce expires amid standoff; Strait of Hormuz to be next focus of rivalry: Chinese expert Global Times 22 Apr 2026 -- US President Donald Trump claimed Tuesday that he will extend the ceasefire with Iran, as the current two-week truce is due to expire on Wednesday night. The move was described by The New York Times as striking a "starkly different tone" from his remarks earlier Tuesday, when he threatened military action if Iran did not agree to US demands.
  • Trump extends U.S. ceasefire with Iran People's Daily 22 Apr 2026 -- U.S. President Donald Trump said Tuesday he will extend the ceasefire with Iran as the current two-week truce is set to expire on Wednesday night.

  • US officials say Iran retains significant military capability despite strikes Press TV 22 Apr 2026 -- Iran continues to maintain substantial military capabilities, despite weeks of US and Israeli strikes, according to multiple US officials familiar with intelligence assessments.
  • Pentagon Intel Believes Iran Retains Considerable Military Might - Reports Sputnik 22 Apr 2026 -- US intelligence believes Iran has retained significant military might, despite America's assetions that Iran's defensive and offensive capabilities have been eliminated, NBC News reports, citing US lawmakers.
  • Iran's Qalibaf Accuses US Of 'Bullying' And Violating Cease-Fire With Blockade RFE/RL 22 Apr 2026 -- Iran's lead negotiator criticized the US blockade of Iranian ports amid uncertainty over the prospects for further peace talks with the United States, reiterating Tehran's claim that the restrictions are a violation of a cease-fire the countries agreed to earlier in April.
  • Aggressors fully responsible for any instability affecting Strait of Hormuz: Iran FM Press TV 22 Apr 2026 -- Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi says the aggressors are fully responsible for any instability affecting the strategic Strait of Hormuz.
  • Violations, threats, blockade main obstacle to genuine talks: President Pezeshkian Press TV 22 Apr 2026 -- The Islamic Republic of Iran has invariably welcomed dialogue and agreement, but Washington's constant breach of commitments, naval blockade and military threats remain the main obstacles to genuine negotiations, President Masoud Pezeshkian said on Wednesday.
  • Military aggression failed, US bullying won't open Strait of Hormuz: Qalibaf Press TV 22 Apr 2026 -- Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Baqer Qalibaf says the country will not open the Strait of Hormuz under bullying from the United States.
  • Don't miscalculate Iran's combat readiness, commander warns enemies Press TV 22 Apr 2026 -- The commander of the Ground Force of the Iranian Army has warned enemies against miscalculating the morale and combat capabilities of Iranian armed forces.
  • Iran's FM says current situation in Strait of Hormuz direct result of US violations Press TV 22 Apr 2026 -- Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi says the prevailing conditions in the Strait of Hormuz are a direct consequence of US violations of international law.
  • Unity of Army, IRGC strategic asset for stabilizing national security: Iran's Army chief Press TV 22 Apr 2026 -- Commander-in-Chief of the Iranian Army Major General Amir Hatami says unity between the Army and the Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) is a strategic asset for maintaining territorial integrity and stabilizing national security.
  • Iran knows how to confront restrictions, stand up to bullying: Araghchi Press TV 22 Apr 2026 -- Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi says Iran knows well how to confront restrictions and defend itself, following the US seizure of an Iranian commercial vessel in the Sea of Oman.

  • Ceasefire extension offers diplomatic opening, but tensions persist in Strait of Hormuz UN News 22 Apr 2026 -- The United States' decision to extend a fragile ceasefire with Iran has kept a narrow window open for diplomacy, but fresh security incidents in the Strait of Hormuz on Wednesday underscore the volatility of the situation and the risks to global shipping and regional stability.

  • Pressing priority is to prevent a relapse in fighting: Chinese FM spokesperson on US' extended ceasefire with Iran Global Times 22 Apr 2026 -- When asked about China's response to US President Donald Trump's extended ceasefire with Iran and what role Chinese diplomacy played in the extension, Guo Jiakun, spokesperson for China's foreign ministry, said that the current situation in the region is at a critical stage of whether the conflict can end. The pressing priority is to prevent by all means a relapse in fighting.

  • Pakistani PM Urges Both Sides In US-Israeli War With Iran To Continue Cease-Fire RFE/RL 22 Apr 2026 -- Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif ⁠on April 22 thanked US President Donald Trump for accepting ⁠his country's request to extend the cease-fire in the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran ⁠to allow diplomatic efforts to continue.

  • UK and France to lead multinational Strait of Hormuz military planning conference UK MOD 22 Apr 2026 -- Military planners from over 30 nations will today (Wednesday 22 April) advance detailed planning to reopen the Strait of Hormuz in a two-day UK-hosted conference.
  • Britain To Host Military Talks On Potential Hormuz Mission Post-Peace Agreement RFE/RL 22 Apr 2026 -- Britain on April 22-23 will host military planners from more than 30 countries for talks starting on a potential multinational mission led by the UK and France to protect sea traffic in the Strait of Hormuz.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • NAVY FY27 Budget Request US Navy 22 Apr 2026 -- single accountable portfolio acquisition executives to speed up delivery of critical capabilities. At the same time, we're restoring discipline and stability in shipbuilding through a disciplined requirements process, smarter contracts using new AI tools, and ensuring predictable, on-time delivery.

  • Statement by Chief Spokesman Sean Parnell on 'Termination of Certain Bargaining Agreements in Accordance with Executive Order 14251' DoD 22 Apr 2026 -- On April 9, 2026, Secretary of War Pete Hegseth signed a memorandum directing the termination of certain collective bargaining agreements in accordance with Executive Order 14251, "Exclusions from Federal Labor-Management Relations Programs." This action ensures Department operations remain aligned with national security requirements and mission readiness.
  • US Army announces new Combat Field Test to enhance Soldier readiness US Army 22 Apr 2026 -- The U.S. Army today announced the implementation of the new Combat Field Test (CFT), a major update to its physical readiness program designed to align fitness standards with the demanding realities of modern combat.
  • Washington, Tennessee National Guard modernizes Bulgarian Land Forces with Strykers Army News 22 Apr 2026 -- With the ongoing transformation of the 81st Brigade from a Stryker to a Mobile Brigade, the Washington National Guard transferred eight Strykers to the Bulgarian Land Forces through the Foreign Military Sales office.
  • Blue Ridge departs Okinawa, Japan U.S. 7th Fleet 22 Apr 2026 -- U.S. 7th Fleet flagship USS Blue Ridge (LCC 19) departed Okinawa following a port visit, April 21, 2026. Blue Ridge's last visit to Okinawa was in 2020.
  • DAF announces next steps in Advanced Nuclear Power for Installations initiative US Air Force 22 Apr 2026 -- The Department of the Air Force, in conjunction with the Defense Innovation Unit, has selected three companies to potentially develop and operate a microreactor on a DAF installation, as part of the Advanced Nuclear Power for Installations initiative.
  • Balikatan 2026: 317th AW conducts MEO, arrives in Philippines US Air Force 22 Apr 2026 -- The 317th Airlift Wing arrived at Clark Air Base, Philippines, to provide critical tactical airlift capabilities following the successful execution of Maximum Endurance Operations, in support of Exercise Balikatan 2026, April 17.
  • DAF releases data, AI strategies to accelerate military dominance US Air Force 22 Apr 2026 -- The Office of the Chief Data and Artificial Intelligence Officer for the Department of the Air Force announced, April 20, the public release of the DAF Data and AI Strategies.
  • Space Force Delivers Final GPS III Satellite to Orbit DoD News 22 Apr 2026 -- The Space Force's Space Systems Command and Combat Forces Command successfully delivered the GPS III-8 satellite into orbit, following its launch from Cape Canaveral Space Force Station, Florida, April 21.
  • GPS III-8 launch succeeds, bolstering national defense and economy as America marks 250th anniversary United States Space Force 22 Apr 2026 -- The U.S. Space Force successfully launched Global Positioning Satellite III-8 on a SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket from Space Launch Complex 40. The mission delivered the final satellite in the GPS III series into orbit. It reinforces the critical role of space-based positioning, navigation and timing in safeguarding the nation and powering its economy as the United States celebrates 250 years since the signing of the Declaration of Independence.
  • US missile stockpiles severely depleted amid terrorist war on Iran: Report Press TV 22 Apr 2026 -- The US military has dangerously depleted its arsenal of critical missiles following its seven-week campaign of aggression against Iran, creating a "near-term risk" of ammunition shortages in any future major conflict, defense experts and sources familiar with internal Pentagon assessments say.
  • 3rd MLR Joins Most Expansive Exercise Balikatan To Date USMC News 22 Apr 2026 -- 3rd Marine Littoral Regiment is contributing more than 1,300 Hawaii-based Marines and Sailors for the 41st iteration of Exercise Balikatan, an expansive multilateral exercise conducted by the Armed Forces of the Philippines and the U.S. military across the Philippine archipelago from April 20 to May 8.

  • NATO Allied Air Command to conduct Exercise RAMSTEIN FLAG 2026 in June NATO Allied Air Command 22 Apr 2026 -- Allied Air Command (AIRCOM) will conduct Ramstein Flag 2026 (RAFL26) from June 8 to June 19, 2026. Now in its third iteration, this large-scale, multi-domain exercise is designed to enhance the Alliance's deterrence and collective defence. Since its inception in 2024, this year marks the first time AIRCOM will lead the entire exercise independently. Operations will take place simultaneously across Northern and Southern Europe within NATO's Joint Operations Areas, combining live-fly missions with synthetic training.
  • NATO Nuclear Symposium concludes in Istanbul NATO 22 Apr 2026 -- NATO's annual Nuclear Policy Symposium concluded in Istanbul on Wednesday (22 April 2026), bringing together 150 experts from across the Alliance to exchange views on nuclear policy and nuclear deterrence ahead of the Ankara Summit.
  • NATO's Special Representative for the Caucasus and Central Asia pays first visit to Armenia NATO 22 Apr 2026 -- On 20-21 April 2026, Ambassador Kevin Hamilton, the NATO Secretary General's new Special Representative for the Caucasus and Central Asia, paid his first visit to Armenia. He was received by President Vahagn Khachaturyan, and held meetings with Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan, Speaker of the National Assembly Alen Simonyan, Foreign Minister Ararat Mirzoyan, Defence Minister Suren Papikyan, and Secretary of the Security Council Armen Grigoryan.
  • NATO Secretary General visits Türkiye in preparation for Ankara Summit, underlines importance of defence industry NATO 22 Apr 2026 -- NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte visited Ankara, Türkiye (21-22 April 2026) to engage with national leadership and visit an ASELSAN Technology Base. In his meeting with President Recep Tayyip Erdoğan, Mr Rutte highlighted Türkiye's contribution to the Alliance and discussed preparations for the upcoming Summit in Ankara.

  • Over UAH 55 Million from Canada: Ukraine Completes First Tallinn Mechanism Cyber Projects Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Ukraine 22 Apr 2026
  • Air defence priorities and joint projects: Ukraine and Spain scale up cooperation Ukraine MOD 22 Apr 2026 -- The Minister of Defence of Ukraine, Mykhailo Fedorov, met with the Minister of Defence of Spain, Margarita Robles. The parties agreed on key areas of cooperation focused on enhancing the defence capabilities of both countries, particularly in air defence.
  • Ukraine to receive a "naval sapper" from the Netherlands: overview of the Alkmaar-class minehunter Ukraine MOD 22 Apr 2026 -- Ukraine will receive the Alkmaar-class minehunter HNLMS Makkum from the Netherlands. Upon joining the Ukrainian Navy, the vessel will be named 'Henichesk' in honour of the ship lost during a combat mission near the Kinburn Spit in June 2022.
  • Ukraine's EU Membership and Training of Ukrainian Pilots on Gripen Aircraft: Meeting with Swedish Delegation Held at the Office of the President President of Ukraine 22 Apr 2026
  • A look at the Defence news 13 - 19 April Netherlands Ministry of Defence 22 Apr 2026

  • China's first Type 076 amphibious assault ship Sichuan heads to S.China Sea for trials and training, showing smooth, rapid progress: expert Global Times 22 Apr 2026 -- China's first Type 076 amphibious assault ship, the Sichuan, has departed Shanghai for the South China Sea for routine cross-regional trials and training, following multiple earlier sea trials and in line with its planned development schedule, the Chinese People's Liberation Army (PLA) Navy said on Tuesday. A Chinese military affairs expert told the Global Times on Wednesday that the Sichuan's first-ever cross-sea-area trial and training deployment to the South China Sea marks rapid and efficient progress, bringing its commissioning one step closer.
  • China's first Type 076 amphibious assault ship heads to South China Sea for regular training Global Times 22 Apr 2026 -- China's first Type 076 amphibious assault ship, the Sichuan, has recently departed Shanghai for the South China Sea to conduct scientific research trials and training exercises, according to the navy.

  • Taiwan signs 6 arms deals with U.S. worth around US$6.6 billion CNA 22 Apr 2026 -- Taiwan has recently signed six major arms procurement deals with the United States worth more than NT$208.77 billion (US$6.6 billion), including High Mobility Artillery Rocket Systems (HIMARS) and M109A7 self-propelled howitzers, the government announced on Wednesday.
  • U.S. envoy meets KMT chair ahead of cross-party arms procurement talks CNA 22 Apr 2026 -- The United States' de facto ambassador to Taiwan met with Kuomintang (KMT) Chairperson Cheng Li-wun (鄭麗文) Wednesday on the eve of planned cross-party negotiations in the Legislature on a stalled defense budget for U.S. arms procurement.
  • Taiwan defense budget crucial: U.S. Indo-Pacific commander CNA 22 Apr 2026 -- The top United States military commander in the Indo-Pacific has warned Taiwan that it must show the resolve to defend itself, including by passing a NT$1.25 trillion (US$39.69 billion) special defense budget, to secure U.S. support.

  • Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's answer to a question from the Moscow. Kremlin. Putin TV programme, Moscow, April 22, 2026 Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 22 Apr 2026

  • Raksha Mantri & his German counterpart hold bilateral talks in Berlin to bolster strategic defence partnership India PIB 22 Apr 2026 -- Raksha Mantri Shri Rajnath Singh held bilateral talks with Germany's Federal Minister of Defence Mr Boris Pistorius in Berlin on April 22, 2026 to further bolster the strategic defence partnership with the European nation. The two Ministers discussed a range of bilateral security and defence issues, including priority areas for co-development & co-production of defence equipment, especially in the field of niche technologies. They also reaffirmed the commitment to enhance military-to-military cooperation as a key pillar of the Strategic Partnership.
  • INS NIREEKSHAK ARRIVES AT COLOMBO, SRI LANKA TO PARTICIPATE IN IN-SLN DIVEX 2026 India PIB 22 Apr 2026 -- INS Nireekshak, the Diving Support and Submarine Rescue Vessel of Indian Navy, arrived at the port of Colombo, Sri Lanka, on 21 Apr 2026 to participate in the 4th edition of IN-SLN DIVEX 2026, a bilateral diving exercise scheduled from 21-27 Apr 2026.

  • Lithuania and Japan team up for world's largest cyber defence Locked Shields 2026 Lithuania MND 22 Apr 2026 -- On April 20-24, one of the largest and most complex cyber defence exercise worldwide, Locked Shields 2026, is organised by the NATO Cooperative Cyber Defence Centre of Excellence (CCDCOE).

  • Northern Ireland start-up boost with new £50 million defence growth deal UK MOD 22 Apr 2026 -- Defence tech start-ups and small businesses in Northern Ireland will benefit from a £50 million investment boost launched by the UK government today, creating highly-skilled jobs and strengthening UK national security.

  • Issues related to the process and development prospects of bilateral cooperation in the defence sector were discussed Ministry of Defence of the Republic of Armenia 22 Apr 2026 -- After the welcoming ceremony of the Minister of Defence of the Republic of Armenia Suren Papikyan, a private meeting between the Ministers of Defence of Armenia and Georgia was launched, which continued with an expanded delegation at the Ministry of Defence of Georgia.

  • Azerbaijan Combined Arms Army's servicemen demonstrate high professionalism during Heydar Aliyev-2026 joint exercise Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 22 Apr 2026 -- In accordance with the military cooperation agreement signed between Azerbaijan and Türkiye, the Distinguished Visitors Day was held within the Heydar Aliyev-2026 joint exercise in Kars, Türkiye, the Ministry of Defense told AZERTAC.

  • EDA and ESA to focus on Earth observation technology for defence European Defence Agency 22 Apr 2026 -- The European Defence Agency (EDA) and the European Space Agency (ESA) signed an arrangement on 22 April to better study space-based Earth observation technology for resilience, security and other defence needs.

  • Defence Minister launches tender to find strategic investor for explosives manufacturer Hexest Materials Estonia MFA 22 Apr 2026 -- Minister of Defence Hanno Pevkur has launched a selective tender to find a strategic investor for Hexest Materials, a state-owned explosives manufacturer, with applications open from interested companies until 25 May 2026.
  • Defence Minister launches tender to find strategic investor for explosives manufacturer Hexest Materials Estonia MoD 22 Apr 2026 -- Minister of Defence Hanno Pevkur has launched a selective tender to find a strategic investor for Hexest Materials, a state-owned explosives manufacturer, with applications open from interested companies until 25 May 2026.
  • Over €700 million awarded from European Defence Fund to projects involving Estonia Estonia MoD 22 Apr 2026 -- A total of 26 international projects with Estonian involvement have been awarded funding in the 2025 Calls for Proposals of the European Defence Fund (EDF).

  • EU-Norway: Joint Press statement on the 5th Security and Defence Dialogue Government of Norway 22 Apr 2026 -- On 21 April, the European Union and Norway held their fifth Security and Defence Dialogue, in Oslo.

  • The Netherlands - Hellfire Missiles US Dept. of State 22 Apr 2026 -- The U.S. Department of State has made a determination approving a possible Foreign Military Sale to the Government of the Netherlands for Hellfire Missiles and related equipment. The estimated total cost is $200 million.

  • Lithuania - AIM-9X Sidewinder Block II Missiles US Dept. of State 22 Apr 2026 -- The U.S. Department of State has made a determination approving a possible Foreign Military Sale to the Government of Lithuania for AIM-9X Sidewinder Block II Missiles and related equipment. The estimated total cost is $214 million.
  • The MoD considers purchasing Merops AS-3 Surveyor test batch Lithuania MND 22 Apr 2026 -- The Government of the Republic of Lithuania has approved the proposal of the Ministry of National Defence to purchase the test batch of Merops AS-3 Surveyor" (AS3/MEROPS) manufactured by Perennial Autonomy.

  • PH Army builds add'l quartering facilities for troops Philippine News Agency 22 Apr 2026 -- The Philippine Army (PA) turned over a two-story modular quartering facility for personnel inside its headquarters in Fort Bonifacio, Taguig City.
  • PH Navy: 'Balikatan' won't be deterred by China's displeasure Philippine News Agency 22 Apr 2026 -- The annual "Balikatan" exercises between the Philippines, the United States, and other like-minded nations will continue despite China's claims that the drills are destabilizing the Indo-Pacific Region.

Defense Industry

  • Contracts for April 22, 2026 DoD
  • Justice Department Seeks to Forfeit Beverly Hills Mansion Purchased with Proceeds of Scheme to Defraud U.S. Military and Bribe an Iraqi Official US Dept. of Justice 22 Apr 2026 -- The Justice Department filed a civil forfeiture complaint today in the U.S. District Court for the Central District of California seeking the forfeiture of a mansion located in Beverly Hills, California, alleged to have been purchased and renovated with approximately $30 million in proceeds of a scheme to defraud the U.S. Department of Defense's Defense Logistics Agency (DLA), pay bribes to an official of the Kurdistan Region of Iraq, and violate U.S. money laundering laws.

  • Hezbollah says it attacked northern Israel in response to ceasefire violations Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 22 Apr 2026 -- Lebanon's Hezbollah group said Tuesday it targeted northern Israel for the first time since a ceasefire took effect, describing the attack as a response to repeated Israeli violations, according to Anadolu Agency.

  • Lebanese MP: Direct negotiations with Israel 'out of question' Press TV 22 Apr 2026 -- A senior Lebanese lawmaker from the political wing of the Hezbollah resistance movement in the parliament has dismissed the notion of direct negotiations between the Beirut government and the Israeli regime.

  • UNRWA Situation Report #7 on the Lebanon Emergency Response 2026 (16 April 2026) UNRWA 22 Apr 2026
  • Statement attributable to the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General - on the death of a peacekeeper in Lebanon UN Secretary-General 22 Apr 2026

    • Other Conflicts

  • Today's top news: Lebanon, Occupied Palestinian Territory, Sudan, Haiti UNOCHA 22 Apr 2026

  • PH's archipelagic defense framework not aimed at China - Navy Philippine News Agency 22 Apr 2026 -- A ranking Philippine Navy (PN) official said the Comprehensive Archipelagic Defense Concept (CADC) is designed to enable the country to defend its maritime domain and is not aimed against China.

  • Afghan Refugees In Qatar Wait For Relocation After US Transfer Deadline Passes RFE/RL 22 Apr 2026 -- Hundreds of Afghan refugees remain in limbo at the As-Sayliyah camp in Qatar awaiting information about the countries they may be transferred to after a US State Department deadline to move them passed last month.

  • Syria Could Be Success Story World Needs, Security Council Hears as UN Officials Outline Strategies to Support Country's Political, Economic Transition UN Security Council 22 Apr 2026 -- Syria could be the success story the world needs, the Security Council heard today, as UN experts outlined how the international community can support that country's political and economic transition.
  • UN Relief Chief urges Security Council to sustain support to Syria as it rebuilds UNOCHA 22 Apr 2026 -- Briefing to the Security Council on the humanitarian situation in Syria by Tom Fletcher, Under-Secretary-General for Humanitarian Affairs and Emergency Relief Coordinator
  • Canada provides funding to the Organisation for the Prohibition of Chemical Weapons to eliminate chemical weapons in Syria Canada 22 Apr 2026 -- Canada is supporting international efforts to identify and eliminate chemical weapons that remain in Syria, to prevent future use.
  • We urge the international community to remain focused on supporting progress towards Syria's economic recovery and long-term stability: UK statement at the UN Security Council UK FCDO 22 Apr 2026 -- Statement by Ambassador James Kariuki, UK Chargé d'Affaires to the UN, at the UN Security Council meeting on Syria.

  • Libya: UN mission chief laments lack of progress towards national renewal UN News 22 Apr 2026 -- Libya's political leaders continue to lag in implementing a roadmap that leads to national elections and unified institutions, the UN Special Representative for the country told the Security Council on Wednesday.
  • Libya's Road Map 'Not Where We Would Like', Mission Chief Tells Security Council, as Speakers Call for Urgent Implementation, Political Will to Break Status Quo UN Security Council 22 Apr 2026 -- The Security Council today broadly agreed on the steps towards Libya's peace and stability — including elections, institutional reunification, security sector reform and economic transparency — but emphasized that the real test will be effective implementation, sustained political will and a decisive break from the status quo.
  • We reiterate our commitment to an inclusive political process which safeguards Libya's unity and stability: UK statement at the UN Security Council UK FCDO 22 Apr 2026 -- Statement by Ambassador Archie Young, UK Deputy Permanent Representative to the UN, at the UN Security Council meeting on Libya.

  • The Republic of Honduras has decided to suspend its recognition of the pseudo "sadr." Morocco Ministry of Foreign Affairs 22 Apr 2026
  • Austria expressed on Wednesday its support for United Nations Security Council Resolution 2797 (2025) affirming that "genuine autonomy under Moroccan sovereignty could constitute a most feasible solution." Morocco Ministry of Foreign Affairs 22 Apr 2026

  • ICC Appeals Chamber confirms jurisdiction in Duterte case ICC 22 Apr 2026 -- On 22 April 2026, the Appeals Chamber of the International Criminal Court ("ICC" or "the Court") confirmed the Court's jurisdiction in the case of The Prosecutor v. Rodrigo Roa Duterte. The Appeals Chamber, by majority, rejected the appeal brought by the Defence and confirmed Pre-Trial Chamber I's decision of 23 October 2025.
  • ICC Appeals Chamber upholds jurisdiction over Duterte case Philippine News Agency 22 Apr 2026 -- The judges of the International Criminal Court (ICC) Appeals Chamber on Wednesday affirmed the ICC's jurisdiction to try former president Rodrigo Duterte for crimes against humanity.

  • Federal Armed Forces to continue their participation in the KFOR peace mission in Kosovo Germany - Federal Government 22 Apr 2026 -- Approved by the Cabinet

News Reports

  • Russia-Seychelles talks President of Russia 22 Apr 2026 -- Vladimir Putin held talks at the Kremlin with President of the Republic of Seychelles Patrick Herminie, who has come to Russia on a working visit.
  • Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's remarks at a reception on the occasion of Orthodox Easter, Moscow, April 22, 2026 Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 22 Apr 2026
  • Bessent Says US Extended Russian Oil Sanctions Waiver Due To Requests From Several Countries RFE/RL 22 Apr 2026 -- Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent says the United States renewed sanctions relief for Russian oil for a month due to requests from several countries that are particularly vulnerable to oil shortages.
  • At the EU Foreign Affairs Council, Latvia raises the issue of barring Russia from participating in the Venice Biennale and imposing sanctions on representatives closely linked to the Russian political regime who use culture to achieve Russia's war aims Latvia MFA 22 Apr 2026 -- Since February 2022, Latvia's total support for Ukraine has exceeded EUR 1 billion, or 0.6% of GDP. More than half of this - EUR 675 million - is military aid, while humanitarian aid and support to the Government of Ukraine amount to EUR 92 million

  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Guo Jiakun's Regular Press Conference on April 22, 2026 PRC MofA 22 Apr 2026
  • China, Mozambique upgrade ties as Xi, Chapo hold talks PRC MND 22 Apr 2026 -- Chinese President Xi Jinping and Mozambican President Daniel Francisco Chapo held talks in Beijing on Tuesday, and the two heads of state agreed to upgrade bilateral ties to a China-Mozambique community with a shared future in the new era.
  • China, Mozambique upgrade ties as Xi, Chapo hold talks People's Daily 22 Apr 2026 -- Chinese President Xi Jinping and Mozambican President Daniel Francisco Chapo held talks in Beijing on Tuesday, and the two heads of state agreed to upgrade bilateral ties to a China-Mozambique community with a shared future in the new era.

  • EU, U.S. lawmakers express concern after Lai's Africa visit postponed CNA 22 Apr 2026 -- The European Union has raised concerns over overflight rights after President Lai Ching-te's (賴清德) planned visit to Eswatini was postponed when three African nations denied overflight clearance for his charter at the last minute.
  • Experts split on possible U.S. rhetoric shift on Taiwan at Trump-Xi summit CNA 22 Apr 2026 -- Taiwanese experts on Tuesday differed on whether the United States might change its rhetoric on "Taiwan independence" at an upcoming U.S.-China summit following Kuomintang (KMT) Chairperson Cheng Li-wun's (鄭麗文) recent meeting with Chinese President Xi Jinping (習近平).
  • China commends relevant countries for upholding one-China principle after Lai's 'transit visit' canceled due to flight permit denial Global Times 22 Apr 2026 -- China's Foreign Ministry and the Taiwan Affairs Office of the State Council on Wednesday both commended relevant countries for upholding the one-China principle after they refused to grant flight clearance for Taiwan regional leader Lai Ching-te's so-called "transit visit" to Eswatini.
  • There is no longer so-called 'ROC president,' anyone who uses the title only brings shame upon themselves: FM on Lai's cancelled trip to Eswatini Global Times 22 Apr 2026 -- The facts are very clear: There is no longer a so-called "ROC president" in the world. Anyone who swindles and deceives others by using such an identity is going against the tide of history and will only bring shame upon themselves, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun said at a press conference on Wednesday.
  • We appreciate relevant countries for upholding the one-China principle: mainland spokesperson on countries denying flight permits for Lai's 'transit visit' Global Times 22 Apr 2026 -- Asked to comment on reports that Taiwan regional leader Lai Ching-te had originally planned to make a so-called "transit visit" to Eswatini, but canceled the trip after relevant countries refused to grant flight clearance to his chartered aircraft, Zhang Han, spokesperson of the Taiwan Affairs Office of the State Council, said on Wednesday that "we appreciate the position and actions of the relevant countries in upholding the one-China principle."
  • China says denial of flight permits to Lai Ching-te shows int'l support for one-China principle People's Daily 22 Apr 2026 -- A Chinese spokesperson on Wednesday said that several countries' refusal to grant flight permits to Taiwan's secessionist-minded leader Lai Ching-te for a planned visit to Eswatini reflects broad international support for the one-China principle.
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 22 Apr 2026
  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson's Remarks on Taiwan Authorities' "Postponement of Lai Ching-te's Visit" to Eswatini Global Times 22 Apr 2026 -- Q: It's reported that Lai Ching-te, leader of the Taiwan authorities, has canceled his trip to Eswatini. The Taiwan authorities said relevant countries abruptly revoked overflight clearance under the pressure from the Chinese mainland. What's the foreign ministry's comment and can you provide more details?
  • Taiwan Affairs Office spokesperson slams Japanese vessel via Taiwan Straits, calls it 'deliberate provocation' Global Times 22 Apr 2026 -- By sending its Self-Defense Force (SDF) vessel into the Taiwan Straits to engage in deliberate provocation, Japan has seriously undermined the political foundation of China-Japan relations and gravely jeopardized China's sovereignty and security. This move once again lays bare the malicious attempt of certain forces in Japan to militarily meddle in Taiwan affairs and sabotage cross-Straits peace and stability, which we firmly oppose, Zhang Han, spokesperson for the State Council Taiwan Affairs Office, said on Wednesday, in response to the recent entry of a Japanese SDF vessel into the Taiwan Straits.
  • We appreciate relevant countries for upholding the one-China principle: mainland spokesperson on countries denying flight permits for Lai's 'transit visit' Global Times 22 Apr 2026 -- Asked to comment on reports that Taiwan regional leader Lai Ching-te had originally planned to make a so-called "transit visit" to Eswatini, but canceled the trip after relevant countries refused to grant flight clearance to his chartered aircraft, Zhang Han, spokesperson of the Taiwan Affairs Office of the State Council, said on Wednesday that "we appreciate the position and actions of the relevant countries in upholding the one-China principle."
  • Mainland spokesperson responds to Taiwan security organs' claim that new cross-Straits measures 'interfere in' island's regional elections Global Times 22 Apr 2026 -- By clinging stubbornly to the separatist "Taiwan independence" stance, the Democratic Progressive Party (DPP) authorities have brought cross-Straits dialogue and consultations to a standstill. They have also deliberately smeared, attacked and suppressed normal cross-Straits exchanges and interactions between the mainland and political parties, organizations and communities from all walks of life in the Taiwan region, Zhang Han, spokesperson for the State Council Taiwan Affairs Office, said on Wednesday.
  • Lai Ching-te's trip to Eswatini cancelled after three African countries revoke flight permits without prior warnings: media Global Times 22 Apr 2026 -- Taiwan regional leader Lai Ching-te cancelled his planned trip to Eswatini after three African countries, Seychelles, Mauritius, and Madagascar revoked its flight permit to cross their countries without prior warnings, according to media reports.

  • Heat maps show largest greenhouse farm in North Korea at less than half capacity Radio Free Asia 22 Apr 2026 -- Months after North Korea unveiled the country's largest-ever greenhouse farm, analysts told Radio Free Asia that satellite imagery shows less than half of the farm is operational, likely due to power shortages.

  • Iran Executes Man Over Alleged Links To Mossad RFE/RL 22 Apr 2026 -- Iran says it has hanged a man after convicting him of alleged espionage and extensive cooperation with the Israeli spy agency Mossad.
  • Mossad agent who sold Iran's sensitive secrets hanged by court order Press TV 22 Apr 2026 -- A Mossad spy, who was responsible for gathering Iran's sensitive information and sending it to the Israeli spy agency, has been executed, according to Iran's Supreme Court.

  • Iran Accused Of Striking Kurdish Opposition Group In Iraq RFE/RL 22 Apr 2026 -- Drone strikes in Iraq's autonomous Kurdistan Region wounded three members of an exiled Iranian Kurdish rebel group on April 22, according to the Kurdistan Freedom Party (PAK), which blamed Iran.

  • EU Unveils Energy Plan Amid Iran War Shock RFE/RL 22 Apr 2026 -- European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen has proposed a strategy to address the consequences of the US-Israeli military war with Iran on European energy markets.

  • PM meeting with Secretary General of the Council of Europe Alain Berset: 22 April 2026 Gov.UK 22 Apr 2026 -- The Prime Minister welcomed the Secretary General of the Council of Europe, Alain Berset, to Downing Street today.

  • Japan's moves on Yasukuni Shrine blatantly provoke international justice, trample on human conscience: FM spokesperson on Japanese PM, officials' offerings at notorious shrine Global Times 22 Apr 2026 -- A series of negative moves by Japan concerning the Yasukuni Shrine blatantly provoke international justice and trample on human conscience; China is deeply outraged by such acts and strongly condemns them, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Guo Jiakun said at a regular press conference on Wednesday.
  • China condemns Japanese PM's consecutive war shrine offerings, calling moves blatant provocation against intl justice, affront to human conscience Global Times 22 Apr 2026 -- China on Wednesday deplored the Japanese prime minister's offerings to ‌the notorious Yasukuni Shrine, where Class-A war criminals from WWII are enshrined, as well as visits by other Japanese officials to the war-linked shrine, calling these moves a blatant provocation against international justice and an affront to human conscience.
  • Japan-New Zealand Summit Telephone Meeting Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 22 Apr 2026

  • Moldovan Oligarch Vladimir Plahotniuc Sentenced To 19 Years In Prison RFE/RL 22 Apr 2026 -- A Moldovan court has sentenced oligarch and former political tycoon Vladimir Plahotniuc to 19 years in prison in a decade-old bank fraud case known as "the theft of the century."

  • Italian PM Meloni Hits Back After Russian TV Host Calls Her A 'Wild Beast' RFE/RL 22 Apr 2026 -- Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni has responded on social media to crude insults made by a bombastic Russian state TV host, writing that "these caricatures certainly won't make us change course."

  • World News in Brief: Insecurity in the Darfurs, 100 million live with landmine threats, Singapore execution moratorium UN News 22 Apr 2026 -- The United Nations is deeply alarmed by continued insecurity in the Darfur region of Sudan and the heavy toll it is having on civilians, Spokesperson Stéphane Dujarric said on Wednesday.
